Package algorithm.gaaco

Class AntColonyOptimization

java.lang.Object
algorithm.gaaco.AntColonyOptimization
public final class AntColonyOptimization extends Object
Ant Colony Optimisation (ACO) stage of the GA→ACO fusion path planner, with concurrent ant tour construction and multiple iteration loops.

Algorithm outline

  1. A fresh PheromoneMatrix is initialised with uniform level τ₀.
  2. A precomputed heuristic matrix heuristic[i][j] = (1/dist[i][j])^β is built once and shared read-only across all ants and all iterations, eliminating repeated Math.pow calls in the hot selection loop.
  3. Ant 1 follows the GA best route verbatim, seeding the pheromone matrix with high-quality trail information before any independent ant explores.
  4. For each of acoIterationCount iterations:
    1. A read-only snapshot of the pheromone matrix is taken; every ant reads from this fixed snapshot without writes — zero contention.
    2. Ants 2 … antCount each build a tour concurrently via an ExecutorService.
    3. After all ants finish, the master thread performs a single batch update: global evaporation, deposits from every ant route (Q / tourLength per edge), plus an elitist deposit from the best route found so far (MMAS-inspired convergence acceleration).
  5. The best route across all ants and all iterations is returned.

Concurrency model — no shared mutable state

  • Each concurrent ant receives its own forked Random, its own boolean[] visited array, and its own local node list — no shared mutable fields between worker threads.
  • The pheromone snapshot (double[][]) and heuristic matrix (double[][]) are read-only after construction; sharing them requires no synchronisation.
  • PheromoneMatrix is only ever accessed by the master thread (ant-1 deposit and batch updates after collection).
  • Route and SensorNode are immutable — safe to pass across thread boundaries via Future.
  • The ExecutorService is created once before the iteration loop and shut down in a finally block after all iterations complete.

Pheromone update semantics

Each iteration uses a batch Ant System update: one global evaporation followed by deposits from all ant routes, then an elitist deposit. This is behaviourally sounder than the legacy per-ant sequential evaporate-and-deposit.

Bug corrections relative to the legacy code

  • B — Reversed pheromone index: consistent snapshot[from][to] where from = current, to = next.
  • D2 — Deposit uses cumulative step distance: fixed to Q / totalTourLength.
  • D6 — Global evaporation missing: evaporateAll(rho) is applied globally after every ant batch.

      public AntColonyOptimization(GaAcoConfig config, Random random)
      Constructs an ACO instance with an externally supplied random source.
      Parameters:
      config - algorithm parameters; must not be null
      random - random source; must not be null

    • AntColonyOptimization

      public AntColonyOptimization(GaAcoConfig config)
      Constructs an ACO instance using an internally created, non-seeded random.
      Parameters:
      config - algorithm parameters; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • refine

      public Route refine(Route gaRoute, List<SensorNode> nodes)
      Refines a GA route using ACO over acoIterationCount iterations and returns the best route found.

      Ant 1 seeds the pheromone matrix with the GA route (sequential, master thread). Each subsequent iteration takes a pheromone snapshot, dispatches antCount − 1 concurrent ants, performs a batch pheromone update, and applies an elitist deposit from the best route found so far.

      Parameters:
      gaRoute - best route produced by the GA stage; must not be null
      nodes - ordered list of all sensor nodes
      Returns:
      best Route found (fitness ≥ gaRoute.getFitness())
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the executor is interrupted during collection
      RuntimeException - if an ant task throws during execution
